title: Surveillance of Lassa fever - Canada.ca
content:
## Surveillance of Lassa fever
Learn how  Lassa fever is monitored.
- Number of cases of Lassa fever in Canada
- Number  of cases of Lassa fever around the world
- How Canada monitors Lassa fever

## Number of cases of Lassa fever in Canada
No cases of Lassa fever have been  reported in Canada.

## Number of  cases of Lassa fever around the world
Lassa fever  was first identified in 1969 in Lassa, Nigeria. In some areas of West Africa,  the disease is endemic, which means it is constantly present.
The number of people infected with  Lassa virus is estimated at between 100,000 and 500,000 each year. There are approximately  5,000 deaths annually.
The majority of cases have been confirmed in:
Cases have also appeared in:
Some cases have been imported to  other countries, such as the United Kingdom and, more recently, the U.S.

## How Canada monitors Lassa fever
All suspected or probable cases of Lassa fever must be immediately reported. Reports  can be made through the 24-hour emergency line at 1-866-262-8433.
